behaviors.description: The traveling-salesman heuristic produces a route no more than 1.5x optimal for inputs up to 20 stops.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the heuristic algorithm itself (pure computation). No I/O, network, OS, or model behavior is required to expose a real failure in route-quality ratio logic.
behaviors.test_description: Run the heuristic on a corpus of deterministic coordinate sets (including adversarial layouts), compute exact optimal tour length with an in-test exact solver for those sets, and assert heuristic_length/optimal_length <= 1.5 for each case.

behaviors.description: Delivery time window estimates are computed correctly from leg distances and configured average speed by road type.
behaviors.justification: Real failures often occur at module boundaries: distance-leg output, road-type mapping, config lookup, and time accumulation. A single-function unit test can miss wiring/config interpretation issues.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real distance-leg calculator output into the time-estimation module with real config loading (local fixture config), then assert per-leg and cumulative time-window values across mixed road types and rounding boundaries.

behaviors.description: Shift-hours validator rejects routes whose total estimated time exceeds remaining shift hours and returns the excess.
behaviors.justification: Core behavior is deterministic arithmetic/threshold validation on provided inputs. No external dependency is required to catch the real failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Call validator with boundary cases (equal to shift, just over by small delta, large overage) and assert reject/accept decision plus exact excess duration returned.

behaviors.description: If one address fails geocoding (no results), the service skips it, optimizes remaining stops, and reports skipped addresses.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on real HTTP/API contract semantics for 'no results' responses. Pure in-process mocks can hide real failure modes (response shape, status/body combinations, transport-level handling).
behaviors.test_description: Run the service against a real HTTP geocoding endpoint (or contract-accurate external test sandbox), include one known unresolvable address among valid ones, and assert: skipped list includes only failed address, optimizer runs on remaining addresses, and output route excludes failed stop.

behaviors.description: Duplicate input addresses are consolidated into one stop with combined delivery items and not visited twice.
behaviors.justification: Likely failure is in orchestration between deduplication, item aggregation, and downstream optimizer input. Unit-only dedup tests can miss duplicate reintroduction in pipeline wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the address-ingestion + consolidation + route-planning path with duplicate addresses carrying different delivery items, then assert one consolidated stop exists, items are merged correctly, and final route contains that stop exactly once.
